free_text: Alpha-induced cross sections of 106Cd for the
Sample type 1, used in Debrecen, Atomki:
backings of 3 micrometer. Target thickness was between
100 and 600 microg/cm2 determined by weight measurement
Sample type 2, used in Notre Dame:
Highly enriched 106Cd 86.4% foil rolled to 2.3 mg/cm2
Sample type 3, natural Cd foil, evaporated onto Al
A Faraday cup to collect the accumulated charge
Some of the bombarding energy were set by Al
Targets were irradiated in Faraday cup from 8.5 to
12.5 MeV alpha beam of 500 nA for 10 h in Debrecen,
and from 7 to 12 MeV alpha beam of 300nA for 8h in
Target stability was checked during and after
Activated targets were measured by HpGe detector.
